page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u2a-c1m2.4xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the ecs.u2a server family. It is built on the KVM hypervisor and features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC processor. The system provides 16 dedicated physical cores with 16 vCPUs, operating at a base clock speed of 2.25 GHz with a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration.

The instance is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio aligns with compute-heavy workloads that require dedicated CPU allocation without the overhead of simultaneous multithreading. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached options. The baseline network bandwidth is rated at 6 Gbps, and the instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Given the dedicated AMD EPYC cores and the 1:2 memory-to-CPU ratio, this server is designed for workloads requiring consistent compute performance. It is suited for tasks such as batch processing, web serving, and distributed analytics where memory footprint is secondary to raw CPU execution.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u2a-c1m2.4xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance within the ecs.u2a family, running on the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated AMD EPYC physical cores operating at 2.25 GHz with a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration, ensuring predictable CPU performance. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, establishing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage options. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 6 Gbps. This hardware profile makes the instance highly suitable for compute-intensive workloads, such as batch processing, web serving, and distributed compute tasks, where dedicated CPU resources are prioritized over large memory allocations.
